task_completion.description: Navigate to Tokyo's Shinjuku district in a map application
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map application is open and centered on Tokyo, specifically the Shinjuku area (look for labels like 'Shinjuku', 'Tokyo', or recognizable geography).
The map interface shows 3D building models rather than flat 2D footprints, or the 3D layer toggle/button is visibly activated in the UI.

task_completion.description: Adjust the camera to a tilted perspective view (45-70 degrees)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is angled, not looking straight down (top-down). The sides and heights of the buildings should be clearly visible, demonstrating depth.
A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing a file named 'tokyo_skyline_3d.png' being saved or existing in the '~/Documents' directory.

task_completion.description: The final image clearly shows Tokyo's urban skyline with 3D building models visible from an elevated, angled viewpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the saved screenshot (either viewed in an image viewer or at the moment of capture) match the requirement: a tilted, 3D perspective of the Shinjuku/Tokyo skyline.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine map application (e.g., Google Earth, Google Maps) rather than a static image
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of interactive map UI elements like zoom controls, search bars, compasses, or layer menus during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download an existing image of Tokyo from the web
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen searching for 'Tokyo 3D skyline' on Google Images or a similar search engine and saving a pre-existing picture.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to download the expected output
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history should not show commands like 'wget' or 'curl' fetching the final image file from an external URL.
